relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Grace must not be received into emptiness.
Now is the welcomed season; now is the day of saving/healing.
Servants are commended through endurance under pressure.
Beatings, prisons, riots, labor, sleeplessness, and fasting mark the path.
Purity, knowledge, patience, kindness, holy Spirit, and sincere love reveal the inner quality.
Truth, Divine power, and justice-weapons accompany the service.
Glory and dishonor, bad report and good report, all become the field.
Unknown yet known, dying yet alive, disciplined yet not killed.
Grieving yet rejoicing, poor yet enriching many, having nothing yet possessing all.
Paul’s mouth opens because his heart is widened.
The constriction is not in him but in their own inward places.
He asks them to become widened too.
Covenant-yoking requires discernment between justice and lawlessness, light and darkness.
Messiah and Belial name incompatible fields of allegiance.
The living Divine dwells and walks among the temple-people.
Separation is framed as being received by the Lord.
The promise becomes family: sons and daughters of the Almighty.

contemplative_note: 2 Corinthians 6 is widened-heart ministry. The servant can be pressured on every side yet inwardly expanded, carrying sincere love and discernment of what can and cannot be yoked.
